highlights.text: Readers know I trade mostly one setup: The Failed Breakdown, and we’ve seen it lots.
Failed Breakdowns start usually when price goes elevator down in a vertical flush cutting through every support.

As always, the gap down will result in a Failed Breakdown/squeeze but I would not hold any size over the weekend.” This was highly prescient, and on early Saturday morning, this risk materialized and we gapped down Sunday 45 points.
Like all gap downs/elevator down sells in ES though, this did not take long to see a Failed Breakdown and rip.
This morning, it failed and we went elevator down to 6715.
In doing so though, ES lost Tuesday’s major 6718 low (which was the low of week) and recovered for a powerful Failed Breakdown.
I provided this yesterday at 4pm: “Watch for the Failed Breakdown of Tuesday’s 6718 daily low.” We ripped from there back to ~6782 which held.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.

post_title: SPX Makes New Lows, But Bounced. Will This Dip Get Bought? March 9 Plan
